programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

Guest starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J (NCIS: Los Angeles)(n)

programme.desc: Part two of two. Agent Callen poses as an arms trade contact in a bid to catch killer Michael Aaron Rivkin. Meanwhile, Tony fears Ziva may not be loyal to the team, and a psychologist uncovers the truth about the relationship between Gibbs and Agent Macy. With Chris O'Donnell, Louise Lombard and LL Cool J(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is forced to work alongside the FBI to catch the killer of an Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent who was murdered during a poker game at the Secretary of the Navy's mansion. Elsewhere, Tony takes on Rivkin in a confrontation that ends in one of them being shot(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team flies to Israel, but receives a hostile reception from Ziva's father, who is the head of intelligence agency Mossad. As events unfold, Gibbs learns that Ziva was romantically involved with Rivkin and finds himself forced to make a tough decision. Guest starring Michael Nouri, alongside Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony travels to North Africa on the trail of missing agent Ziva, who is thought to have been tracking down a terrorist leader called Saleem Ulman. Intent on exacting revenge for her apparent death during a flight to Somalia, he is taken hostage by militants and discovers she is still alive - only to be given a potentially catastrophic ultimatum. Starring Mark Harmon and Cote de Pablo(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team is asked to investigate when three convicts break out of prison, including a brilliant hacker who was forced to help the other criminals steal billions of dollars online. Meanwhile, Paige struggles to manage Ralph's expectations when his father returns. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A musical prodigy assists Scorpion in investigating the murder of his friend, who was a music industry blogger. Meanwhile, Drew takes Ralph to a baseball game and later visits the headquarters to seek Walter's advice in a bid to help him connect with his son. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when an Islamic terrorist group in Iraq steals a truck with $5million and track the money back to America. The agents learn the cash has been used to set up a bank robbery intended as a ruse to allow a bank employee to transfer $100million of the terrorists' money that has been frozen by the government in an offshore bank. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval officer involved in an illegal underground street race is killed when his vehicle explodes. The team's investigations reveal he was carrying classified equipment in relation to his work on a new form of battery that the military had hoped to adopt, and that the explosion - captured on CCTV - is not in accordance with that expected of normal fuel. Meanwhile, Callen latest driving misdemeanour means he must attend traffic school. Chris O'Donnell stars(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine gunnery sergeant Wendell Dobbs is gunned down in a drive-by shooting by assailants wielding AK-47s, and the team suspects the victim may have been involved in illegally importing automatic weapons into the country and supplying them to local gangs. Sam goes undercover to find the truth after learning Dobbs gave up his combat post to mentor his younger brother, who had been hanging out with a gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Daniel Zuna is found dead in a pool of his own blood in an alleyway outside a night club, and the team's first stop is to investigate the mixed martial arts gym where the victim spent most of his off-duty time. Kensi poses as Zuna's new girlfriend and suspects the murder could be related to something that happened in Iraq, prompting Sam to go undercover as an MMA fighter in order to see if he can find out the truth. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J and Daniela Ruah(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Having discovered Irene Radford is back, Gina is determined to put her old enemy behind bars once and for all - and the two are soon face to face again when an intruder at the Radford house turns out to be Irene herself, who claims she has returned because she is critically ill with cancer. However, the police have got wind that she is plotting one final raid before retiring from a life of crime for good, so Phil is tasked to monitor a prison meeting between Irene and her son David, where the top brass are hoping he'll glean valuable information about the job. Unfortunately, the philandering DS is easily distracted by an old flame working at the prison and misses half the conversation - so when the raid gets under way, it soon becomes clear they've been duped, and a senior officer ends up staring down the barrel of Irene's gun. Back at the station, Honey is upset to learn that Amber has taken the 'shy and sensitive' Steve Hunter's virginity, while Sam seems to be warming to Phil. Is ro(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Following the Radford family's botched attempt to spring David from prison, Irene drives off with Gina at gunpoint and forces the inspector to let her colleagues know she means business. If they don't give up the chase, she won't hesitate to pull the trigger. Soon, Gina finds herself alone with her enemy, and as they begin to talk, the two of them realise they understand one another better than they could possibly have imagined. However, when Irene orders the shooting of Jack Gaunt, it becomes clear Gina is still in very real danger. Meanwhile, Honey has it out with Steve, disappointed that he had sex with Amber, and accusing him of being as bad as his brother. Indeed, Phil is having his fair share of problems as Sam discovers that his New Year's invitation followed hard on the heels of his dalliance with the prison liaison officer. Roberta Taylor, Lynda Bellingham, Lisa Maxwell and Scott Maslen star(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Staff shortages cause tension in CPU, with Ramani bearing the brunt as she struggles to cope with an increased workload - and when an outside source brings the matter to Adam's attention, the situation becomes hugely political, only serving to aggravate the already volatile relationship between Jack and Neil. By way of an immediate solution, Jack assigns Terry to help Ramani with the case of a man suspected of being a paedophile - but Ramani is worried about Terry's ability to deal with such a task given his own traumatic background. Meanwhile, the rest of the relief let their hair down at Lance's stag party - but when the guest of honour nips outside for a breath of fresh air, he is suddenly attacked from behind by a group who put a bag over his head and bundle him into the back of a van. It could just be a stag night prank - or it could be something more serious. Guest starring Annabelle Apsion and Colm O'Maonlai(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Lance is missing on his wedding day, as friends try to 'cure' him of his homosexuality. Terry tries to prove his suspicions that brother Ben is a child abuser, while no-nonsense new DC Jo Masters arrives at Sun Hill to defuse the tension between Jack and Neil - but proves to be their worst nightmare(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth disguises herself as a battered wife in an attempt to find out who is threatening the future of a women's refuge. Hetty's quest to find the leak opens her eyes to the terrifying realities of spousal abuse, and results in a gripping conclusion as she leads the women in defending their sanctuary against a violent motorcycle gang(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: When her niece's son is charged with possession of an ecstasy tablet, suspicions remain that he has been framed, so Hetty goes undercover in his school as a cafeteria worker to clear his name. Patricia Routledge stars(n)

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's Colour Blind
programme.desc: Drama from the best-selling author. Set between the wars, it concerns a girl who returns to her Tyneside home newly married to her black husband, only to encounter bigotry from all sides, to say nothing of her obsessed brother's animosity. Niamh Cusack, Tony Armatrading and Dearbhla Molloy star(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Poplar is gripped by an outbreak of dysentery, but that's the least of mum-to-be Marie Amos's worries when her husband Tony is arrested for gross indecency in the gentlemen's toilets and faces a criminal hearing. Nurse Crane meets Dolores McEvoy, a pregnant Irish woman who is clearly malnourished - but when she visits the woman's home, it turns out she has given a false address and has disappeared. Trixie is delighted when Tom asks her to oversee her first official church duty since their engagement, while at Nonnatus House, Fred tackles a rat infestation - only for Sister Monica Joan to work against him. With guest appearances by Cara Theobold (Downton Abbey) and Richard Fleeshman (Coronation Street)(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Winifred's eyes are opened to Poplar's seedy underworld when she cares for expectant prostitute Bridget Cole. Horrified by the young woman's lack of awareness, she draws on her teaching experience, and with help from Sgt Noakes sets out to promote safe sex to all local working girls. Barbara struggles to understand why expectant father Frank Robbins would pressure his wife to bear him a son, while Sister Monica Joan is feeling sidelined - until she finds herself unexpectedly assisting Shelagh with an emergency birth. Trixie invites herself along to a meeting between Tom and the Bishop - but things get tense when the idea of the couple moving to Newcastle is raised(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry celebrates his birthday at a comedy club, where a controversial act comes under fire from a group of hecklers. Sandra discovers the most vocal member of the gang is the niece of a political activist who went missing 25 years previously after waging a campaign against the comedian. Agreeing to reinvestigate the disappearance, the squad discovers that the activist and her boyfriend had infiltrated an extreme right-wing group with links to the dangerous criminal who murdered Jack's wife. Guest starring Phil Daniels, Joe Absolom and John Henshaw(n)

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Kenneth Branagh stars as the world-weary Swedish detective in a fourth and final series of adaptations of Henning Mankell's novels. Wallander travels to Cape Town to investigate the disappearance of a Swedish woman. He uncovers a complex conspiracy behind the mystery, and is also confronted with a nation that has reached a turning point in its history(n)

However, his awkward nature proves an obstacle to winning her over, despite the sage advice of his worldly-wise little sister. Comedy, starring John Gordon Sinclair, Dee Hepburn, Jake D'Arcy, Clare Grogan and Robert Buchanan(n)

She then leaves home and finds work at a dairy farm, where she falls in love with Angel Clare and agrees to marry him. However, the bride-to-be suffers an attack of conscience the night before her wedding and decides to tell her prospective husband about her past. Gemma Arterton stars(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures - Everton v Crystal Palace at Goodison Park and Brighton & Hove Albion v Watford at AMEX Stadium. Everton were unbeaten in their last nine meetings with the Eagles, but the sides could not be separated when they met at Selhurst Park in August. programme.title: Songs of Praise

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson goes property hunting in rural Wiltshire with a couple who have £950,000 to buy a family home with land, where they and their young children can enjoy a self-sufficient lifestyle. Jules also takes time out to find out about the county's glove-making heritage and sees some famous examples made for famous figures(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v Italy (Kick-off 3.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the concluding match from the second round of fixtures, held at Stade de France. The hosts will be in confident mood after their impressive performance against England in their opening match, while Italy were comfortably routed by Wales. The French prevailed 25-14 in Italy last year to extend their winning run against the Azzurri in this competition to six matches. With analysis from Thomas Castaignede, Sergio Parisse and Joe Worsley, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Jonathan Davies and Paul O'Connell(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Cwmcarn, south Wales, Kim and Simon's steep garden is little more than three huge, muddy tiers, so Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich set out to turn it into a relaxing space worthy of the area's amazing views(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker, Charlotte Smith and Steve Brown head to the Auchlyne Estate near Killin in Perthshire, the first of four visits they will be making over the next year to chart life there throughout the seasons. Plus, Tom Heap goes back to the classroom to see if the next generation of farmers are being given the training they need(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: From ancient Syria to present day Sheffield and out into the wilds of space, something is stalking the Doctor and her friends. As Graham, Yaz and Ryan return home to see friends and family, they find themselves haunted by very different experiences. Who is the figure calling from beyond the stars for help, and what are the fearsome Chagaskas terrorising Aleppo in 1380? To find the answers, the Doctor and her companions must embark on a mission that forces them to face their darkest fears(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: At the Turners, Shelagh receives a phone call from the Hong Kong adoption agency, which has the alarming news that someone from May's past has arrived in the UK and wants to see the little girl. Meanwhile, Valerie's cousin Maureen is heavily pregnant and hoping her baby will be born on Thursday, the same date as her father and grandfather. However, her due date isn't so soon and keen to bring on the birth, Maureen takes a big risk by secretly taking castor oil - with potentially disastrous consequences(n)

programme.title: The Pale Horse
programme.desc: Widowed antique dealer Mark Easterbrook investigates when his name appears on a list found in the shoe of a dead woman, and his enquiries lead him to the home of three spinsters in Much Deeping. The village is a place of old traditions, macabre rituals and strange beliefs, a place of witches, curses and spells, and as more deaths occur Mark starts to fear for his own life and sanity. First of a two-part adaptation of Agatha Christie's 1961 novel, starring Rufus Sewell, Sean Pertwee and Kaya Scodelario(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Manchester City v West Ham United at Etihad Stadium and Sheffield United v Bournemouth at Bramall Lane. West Ham were looking to avenge a 5-0 thrashing at the hands of the Citizens on the opening weekend of the season, while Sheffield United were aiming to continue their impressive campaign back in the top flight by inflicting more misery upon the struggling Cherries(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: A bedridden Jake falls for Willow and finds himself peddling her brand of snake oil, while things might be hotting up between Bear and Ayesha(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu are hired to investigate a fire at an award-winning salon, with owner Odette convinced that her old friend and now rival Isobel is behind the blaze and a number of other acts of sabotage. Guest starring Sally Lindsay and Jacqueline Boatswain(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple looking for a home in Oxfordshire on a budget of £1.5 million, and also braves the county's waters during an exhilarating attempt at punt racing(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Inner-city farm manager Tom showcases his sheep at the Lambeth Country Show, while beekeeper Paul strives to sell his produce to support local charities close to his heart(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tim Medhurst and Christina Trevanion scour south-west England for items to sell at auction, with Tim blowing more than half his budget on rare military memorabilia(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Inside Out London
programme.desc: The programme finds out what is being done to help railway staff developing serious mental health problems because of violent passengers. Plus, a woman who has found a unique way to help people with dementia(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Callum prepares for his assessment and gets much-needed reassurance from Karen and Stuart - only to be thrown by the arrival of an unexpected visitor. Linda and Mick put on a united front with the social worker, and Sonia invites Whitney on a weekend away - unaware Leo is eavesdropping from the attic. Bobby suggests that Bex should move in with him, Iqra and Habiba, and Rainie gives Daniel a piece of her mind about his fake funeral plans(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Callie Lewis was just 24 when she took her own life. She had been in the care of the NHS, but fell through the cracks of an understaffed and overstretched system. For the last 16 months, Panorama has been filming with her family as they tried to understand what went wrong. Shortly before she died, they learned that Callie was a member of an online suicide forum, where she found detailed advice on how to kill herself. As reporter Ellie Flynn discovers, Callie's death comes at a time when many people with mental health issues continue to complain about their struggle to connect with the services they need(n)

programme.title: Rio and Kate: Becoming a Stepfamily
programme.desc: Documentary following former England captain Rio Ferdinand and his fiancee Kate Wright in the months leading up to their wedding as she integrates into the family and becomes a stepmother to his three children Lorenz, Tate and Tia. Kate takes on not only the role as Rio's new partner, but also of parent to three children still grieving over the death of their mother Rebecca in 2015 and grandmother Janet two years later(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam lies to Jess about missing his mental-health assessment and tensions in their relationship reach breaking point, leaving him suddenly nostalgic for the simplicity of first love(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic 400m gold medallist Christine Ohuruogu, Cricket World Cup-winner Mark Wood, Sheffield United striker Billy Sharp and Great Britain swimming star Fran Halsall join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets Hollywood star Mark Ruffalo, who is currently appearing in new legal thriller Dark Waters. Friends star David Schwimmer and British comedian and writer Nick Mohammed discuss their new spy comedy Intelligence. Episodes star Tamsin Greig also appears to talk about her role in Julian Fellowes' new period drama, Belgravia. Plus, there's music from singer-songwriter Alicia Keys, who performs her new single Underdog, while more intrepid audience members attempt to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley reports on a millionaire who let the public fund his car racing hobby, and the boss of a recycling firm who helped himself to millions in government money(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore a former mill in Preston, a canalside cottage in Stourport-on-Severn and a terraced house in Chiswick(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Mum Rachael thinks she and her son Harry should move to Australia for a better quality of life. Harry, however, is not so sure, so the pair spend a trial week in Brisbane(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Fly-tippers are caught on camera loading their truck with 150 bags of waste before dumping it in the Rhondda Valley, and a training centre for illegal dog fighting is raided(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Tim Weeks and Colin Young help two teams find items at an antique centre in Elsecar, South Yorkshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp learns about the history of local landscape with artist David Walker Barker(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: A grieving mother takes the law into her own hands to get justice for her son, and Karen thwarts Al and Bear's plans to make the Mill's system automatic(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: An energy tycoon hires Frank and Lu to keep an eye on his daughters, one of whom is going off the rails and the other is about to get married - but things quickly get out of hand(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps two teachers find the ideal country home in Lancashire for their four children, and also pays a visit to one of the last working forges in the country(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Pig farmer Keith and budding herb grower Peter battle the temperamental British weather to get their premium produce to families and friends at Blackheath Farmers' Market(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Tim Medhurst continue in the south west of England, where Christina goes caving to uncover the little-known story of maturing cheddar cheese(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda berates Mick for talking to the social worker about her and sets out to put the woman straight, while Tina questions whether Ollie is safe around his mother, leaving Mick conflicted. Whitney opens up to Kush about a letter she kept from Tony, leaving Leo intrigued as he listens in. Sonia is distraught to learn that Bex is moving out, Sharon and Dennis finally reach an understanding and Jean is upset when Daniel snaps at her(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Cameron plans to give Chloe his mother's necklace as a Valentine's Day gift - but first he needs to get it back from Nicky. She has problems of her own - she's pawned Bernie's necklace to pay her mother's debts, but is still being harassed for more money, forcing her to put her life and career on the line. Sacha is troubled by a personal decision, but receives guidance from an old friend(n)

programme.title: The Split
programme.desc: Return of the legal drama, starring Nicola Walker. A death in the family brings everyone back together. While Hannah's marriage seems to be back on track, she has begun a dangerous affair with Christie. Zander returns from America, and introduces an unsettling new addition to the team. Nathan takes on a protegee, and the firm takes on two high-profile clients - a TV presenter locked in an unhappy and controlling marriage and a man who wants a divorce before he dies. Guest starring Donna Air(n)

programme.title: Plastic Surgery Undressed
programme.desc: Four women watch live breast reduction as they consider going under the knife. They share their reasons for thinking about surgery, many of which are often more practical than aesthetic, including back pain and limitations on what they can do with their children(n)

programme.title: Hindus: Do We Have a Caste Problem?
programme.desc: Parle Patel investigates the hereditary class system of Hindu society, asking what kind of impact does it have on other Hindus living in Britain. He meets a young woman who is so proud of her caste she is still working in the same occupation as her ancestors did thousands of years ago, and also speaks to a man who has rejected his religion because he believes it's the only way to escape his caste(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Danny Clarke transform a dank Victorian garden in Sunderland into an inviting space for the owner's grandchildren(n)

programme.title: The Edible Garden
programme.desc: Gardeners' World presenter Alys Fowler demonstrates how to grow salad ingredients in the garden, including tomatoes, lettuce and cucumbers, but a blight hampers proceedings. She then prepares lime-leaf salad, dandelion fritters and nettle beer(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Countryfile presenters Adam Henson and Steve Brown team up with antique experts David Harper and Roo Irvine for a trip around the rolling Cotswold Hills, hoping to find items that will make the biggest profit at auction. The competing duo also explore the area's history, with Adam discovering the vital work a charity played saving horses during the Great War and Steve taking a trip down a canal to learn how a little boat save hundreds of lives at Dunkirk(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Dungeness in Kent, where Margherita Taylor meets people behind a campaign to save film director Derek Jarman's famous cottage and garden and spends time with the last of the hardy women who helped launch lifeboats by hand in former times. Matt Baker explores the town's strange and barren landscape and chats to people harvesting sloes for a very special gin festival. Guest presenter Deborah Meaden looks at what the future holds for rural post offices(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers cook comfort food dishes straight from the oven, including a new take on steak and chips and sticky gingerbread(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Scientist Andrea Turkalo examines the social bonds formed by elephants deep in the rainforests of central Africa. After more than 20 years of field study, she has begun to unravel the secrets of the animals' lives and the meanings of the sounds they use to communicate with each other. Richard Armitage narrates(n)

programme.title: Flatpack Empire
programme.desc: Cameras go behind the scenes of IKEA, one of the world's most successful, enigmatic and recognisable global brands. It begins with a look at how the company is starting to open up its design headquarters to outside influences. British designer Tom Dixon has been invited to collaborate on a new bed-sofa, but it proves a source of disagreement(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Piers Taylor and Caroline Quentin explore bold and extraordinary homes in Spain, a country renowned for experimental and fearless architecture. They include a metal home in the foothills of the Pyrenees that is more like a piece of art, an architect's home in Madrid made of seven vast concrete and steel beams and a space-age circular building in the wilderness of Aragon(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Exploring the relationship between director Alfred Hitchcock and his stars, featuring interviews with the man himself, Kim Novak, Tippi Hedren, Joan Fontaine, Janet Leigh and Sean Connery. Sylvia Syms narrates(n)

programme.title: To Catch a Thief (1955)
programme.desc: A retired cat burglar becomes the prime suspect for a series of robberies carried out in his own distinctive style, and sets out to clear his name by catching the copycat crook responsible. His quest takes him to the Cote d'Azur, where he encounters the demure daughter of a wealthy holiday-maker, and before long the two are drawn together. Alfred Hitchcock's Oscar-winning romantic mystery, starring Cary Grant, Grace Kelly and Jessie Royce Landis(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Philip Serrel and Catherine Southon find intricately carved walnuts and a silver eperne at Bletchley Park, Buckinghamshire, while Paul Martin meets Second World War codebreakers(n)

programme.title: Undiscovered Worlds with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The explorer sets out to become the first person to reach the summit of a mountain in Greenland's Arctic wilderness. This already dangerous expedition is made all the more treacherous by the impact of climate change, as rising temperatures in the Arctic cause the ice sheets to melt. He must also brave rivers of freezing meltwater, quicksand, and treacherous glacial ice(n)

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Women's downhill skiing action from the World Cup meeting at Garmisch Partenkirchen. Plus, a look at snowboarding group the No Medals Crew as they film their latest movie(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris are sent on a high-altitude mountain expedition across Peru in second-hand American cars. Plus, back at the track, Chris tests out the latest electric car from Porsche(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: As winter draws in and the weather grows wilder, the remaining couples are challenged to clear a path through a dense area of the wilderness for a dog sled team in just three hours. Once the work is completed, the captain of the sled team selects one of the couples to visit Ose Mountain, where they must clear debris from a reservoir and learn how to correctly butcher an animal in the wild(n)

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life - including Romesh's mother - are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today and the latest talking points in the media. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by a selection of celebrity guests, comedians and experts, to investigate how and why the events in the news matter to people across the country(n)

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's Tour of Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian explores how Scotland's landscape has shaped the nation's history. He begins on a trek from Aberdeen to Oban, meeting a hermit called Jake and exploring his very unique treehouse. He then visits the community of Findhorn, finding out how this remote area of barren sand dunes became transformed into a thriving village whose main aim is to be environmentally, economically and socially self-sustaining. Frankie also views one of the most stunning glens in the Highlands, giving his perspective on the Glencoe massacre of 1692, before going deep underground to visit the Cruachan Power Station(n)

programme.title: Nightcrawler (2014)
programme.desc: A cynical petty crook is inspired when he sees a freelance camera crew, and decides to go into business filming crime scenes and accidents, then selling the footage to news broadcasters. His determination to stay ahead of the competition leads him down a dark path as his methods become increasingly ruthless. Crime drama, starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Rene Russo and Bill Paxton(n)

programme.title: The Unknown Girl (2016)
programme.desc: After failing to open the door to a woman who later turns up dead, a guilt-stricken young medical doctor turns amateur detective. Belgian-French thriller, starring Adèle Haenel, Olivier Bonnaud, Jérémie Renier and Louka Minnella. In French(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Harpenden, St Albans, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties enter the contest in St Albans - a converted farmyard barn with a modern twist, a zero-carbon self-build and a 1950s Scandinavian-style bungalow(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sacha prepares to perform a groundbreaking bowel transplant operation, but Max fears he is the wrong man for the job, given his recent bereavement and past struggles with mental health. Nicky is struggling to keep her debts under control, only to be confronted with her mother as a patient, who reveals that she now owes even more money. Donna encounters a casting director, and tries to get herself on his books(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Nicola West and Jake Andrews dream of giving their little daughter Auri the best start in life they can, even if it means leaving the UK. However, Nicola is scared of hurting her loved ones by taking her daughter far away. Nicki Chapman offers the pair a week in Brisbane to check out the homes, jobs and lifestyles on offer, to help them decide whether to make a move once and for all(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: There's a 1970s theme prior to Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram's last auction in Cambridge, as Raj weighs in with a large set of standing scales and Natasha shells out on a lazy Susan. Natasha also steps aboard a narrowboat antiques shop moored on a canal, while Raj rummages in the den of a pair of foxes and emerges with perhaps his most unpredictable purchase to date(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The search for a chef to be crowned MasterChef: Professionals champion 2019 is almost over, as the three finalists face one last challenge. They are given three hours to cook a starter, a main course and a dessert, each of which takes their culinary abilities to a whole new level, with flawless presentation and exciting flavours. Judges Marcus Wareing, Monica Galetti and Gregg Wallace assess their efforts, before deciding the winner(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Lauren Jenkins presents action from the second round of fixtures, featuring France v Italy at Stade Beaublanc, Scotland v England at Scotstoun and Ireland v Wales at Energia Park(n)

programme.title: Walks of Life
programme.desc: Mehreen Baig explores the Lake District in a spiritual journey inspired by the work of poet William Wordsworth, beginning with a visit to a holy shrine only accessible at low tide(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott chooses four dishes that really push things to the limit, each providing a technical challenge that could inspire viewers to tackle something new. The menu includes the Hairy Bikers' sushi, James Martin's crown of lamb, Raymond Blanc with his lobster with red pepper and cardamom jus, and the ultimate chocolate cake from Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Everyone deals with aftermath of the fire, with Laser Byrne seeking retribution and Rory pushing to be appointed Nikki's replacement(n)

programme.title: Patagonia: Earth's Secret Paradise
programme.desc: A glimpse at the rarely seen South American wilderness, home to a range of creatures from terrain as varied as the Andes to Cape Horn. Cameras reveal the secret lives of pumas and hummingbirds, soar with condors over glacial peaks and explore monkey puzzle forests dating back to the time of the dinosaurs. The extreme kayakers who gain thrills and excitement from traversing the area's raging waterfalls are observed, as are the native cowboys, known as gauchos, as they round up wild horses. Narrated by Santiago Cabrera(n)

programme.title: Wild UK
programme.desc: Zoologist Lucy Cooke and wildlife cameraman Colin Stafford-Johnson explore the nation's wildernesses to reveal the wildlife that exists there, beginning with its rivers(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and David Harper assess items brought into Hull, while Paul Martin hears the story of the local women who campaigned for a change in the safety laws on trawlers(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman, Vick Hope, Gethin Jones and Tim Vine go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through Indonesia, starting out from the capital Jakarta and travelling south to the city of Bogor, where he admires its botanical gardens(n)

programme.title: The Twinstitute
programme.desc: Doctors Chris and Xand van Tulleken explore two methods of quitting smoking, with the help of 23-year-old identical twins Gareth and Mason from London, whose 10-a-day habit has led to their lungs resembling those of a 40-year-old. Chris has arranged for Mason to spend a month undertaking an intensive course of cognitive behavioural therapy, which was pioneered in the 1960s to help patients understand what are known as 'automatic thoughts'. Meanwhile, Xand introduces Gareth to acupuncture, which is no longer available on the NHS as a smoking treatment, but there is evidence that shows it does work for some people(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contenders to brave the black chair, answering questions on the specialist subjects of WH Auden, UK rap music, Louis Braille, and 1970s and ‘80s British sitcoms. They then each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell asks the questions as the Lexplorers take on the Suits in the first of the quarter-final matches, with a place in the semi-finals at stake. They must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between groups of four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Aristotle, Johannes Kepler, Isaac Newton, and Isaac Asimov(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the third of the quarter-finals, two teams of four students compete to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Mary Beard's Shock of the Nude
programme.desc: Part two of two. The classicist looks at an eclectic range of works to show how artists have challenged the idea of 'the body beautiful', and explores how artistic nudes can provoke viewers to consider fundamental questions about being human. Mary reflects on Marc Quinn's sculpture of artist Alison Lapper, Sally Mann's images of her own children in the 1990s and the controversy that surrounded their exhibition, and examines society's shifting perception of gender, from the binary of male and female to a more fluid concept(n)

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Young couple Beattie and Sam are finally on the property ladder, but the only flat they can afford has a dark and sinister past - one that demands to be heard. Jenna Coleman and Kadiff Kira star in Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith's comic anthology(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Sarah Smith(n)

programme.title: The Bank That Almost Broke Britain
programme.desc: The story of how RBS, a small Scottish bank, grew to briefly become the biggest in the world, before collapsing and triggering the largest financial bail-out in British history. This documentary focuses on a single day, the 7th of October 2008, when the Royal Bank of Scotland collapsed and almost took the entire UK banking system down with it. Featuring interviews with then-Chancellor of the Exchequer Alistair Darling, former BBC Business Correspondent Robert Peston and Mervyn King, Governor of the Bank of England at the time(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Four groups of dedicated farmers who have shown resilience and ingenuity, go up against each other bidding to win this year's Against the Odds accolade at the Annual British Farming Awards. Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Dungeness in Kent, where Margherita Taylor meets people behind a campaign to save film director Derek Jarman's famous cottage and garden and spends time with the last of the hardy women who helped launch lifeboats by hand in former times. Matt Baker explores the town's strange and barren landscape and chats to people harvesting sloes for a very special gin festival. Guest presenter Deborah Meaden looks at what the future holds for rural post offices(n)

programme.title: Keeler, Profumo, Ward and Me
programme.desc: Tom Mangold shares his experiences of reporting first-hand on the Profumo scandal, and shares the interviews he has conducted with several of the key players, including an exclusive audiotape interview with Stephen Ward, recorded two weeks before his suicide in 1963. Mangold shares his memories of Ward, whom he believes was unfairly scapegoated, and presents extracts from interviews conducted with both Christine Keeler and Mandy Rice-Davies, which have never been broadcast(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Hornetts sample a week in Brisbane and while Simon has dreamt of making it his home, his wife Joanna has strong emotional ties to the UK that she would find very hard to break(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams search for bargains at Wetherby Racecourse, while Charlie Ross learns the history of an unusual chapel in Knaresborough that is hundreds of years old and carved into rock(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team transforms a home in Bolton, Lancashire for the Taylor-Mann family, making the house more suitable for nine-year-old William, who has severe autism. Williams's condition means that he is extremely hypersensitive to noise, and being in close proximity to his siblings is often just too much for him to bear. Nick Knowles and the team, with the help of kind volunteers, transform the property to create a quiet, calm space that can help William feel as comfortable as possible(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including Hull FC v Hull Kingston Rovers at KCOM Stadium, Warrington v St Helens at Halliwell Jones Stadium and Castleford Tigers v Wigan Warriors at Mend-a-Hose Jungle(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team search for ideal locations for seaside leisure, where they can enjoy their personal passions by the coast(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: Alex Jones talks to Brian Conley about her small-screen favourites, while reminiscing about her childhood. The One Show presenter re-enacts scenes from her favourite kids' show, reveals which popular comedian terrified her as a youngster and how a mix-up led to her big break. She also discusses her family's love of hidden-camera shows and how the family cat made the telly explode(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Keith needs an alibi for the arson, but hates his dad's solution. Sharon picks up a small-time dealer, who Laser later targets(n)

programme.title: Patagonia: Earth's Secret Paradise
programme.desc: The documentary offering a glimpse into the rarely seen South American wilderness continues, this time following the path of the relentless wind sweeping east through Patagonia's dry desert from the Andes' peaks. The programme reveals a weird world of maras - a large relative of the guinea pig - and penguins, and witnesses the first faltering steps of baby guanacos - Patagonia's very own camels. Cameras also observe the people who have taken on this inhospitable environment and carved out a home for themselves. Santiago Cabrera narrates(n)

programme.title: Wild UK
programme.desc: Zoologist Lucy Cooke and wildlife cameraman Colin Stafford-Johnson explore the nation's forests, and see how the pine marten in Scotland is helping animals in Wales(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques expert Elizabeth Talbot discovers a valuable sculpture at Rochester Cathedral in Kent and Raj Bisram finds a ghostly Victorian photo album(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman, Vick Hope, Gethin Jones and Tim Vine go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the largest island of the Indonesian archipelago, travelling through rainforests and paddy fields from the capital, Jakarta, to the volcanic region at the heart of Java's Central Province. He visits Java's most dangerous volcano, Mount Merapi, learns how to make noodles, and visits the Borobudur Temple, a Buddhist shrine that lay buried under a layer of volcanic ash for centuries(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris are sent on a high-altitude mountain expedition across Peru in second-hand American cars. Plus, back at the track, Chris tests out the latest electric car from Porsche(n)

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: The largest boats in the fishing fleet head out to sea in autumn, including giant netters and trawlers up to 100 feet long capable of fishing hundreds of miles from shore. On one boat, an engineer and deckhand with 10 years of service behind him is training to become a captain, under the watchful eye of his veteran skipper. The vessel faces a rough week at sea in gale force winds. Back on land, the owner of a shellfish company that employs workers from Latvia and Lithuania fears for the future as Britain leaves the EU(n)

programme.title: Universal Credit: Inside the Welfare State
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at Liverpool's busiest jobcentre in Toxteth. Among new applicants for Universal Credit is a woman made redundant at 62, who is under huge pressure to find work. A long-standing veteran of jobcentres reveals how the new benefits system is struggling to accommodate everyone, and a pregnant woman who came to Liverpool from the Czech Republic as a child is made to take a test to prove that she is a long-term resident in the city. Elsewhere, cameras follow the senior civil servant responsible for Jobcentres as he struggles to cope with moving 100,000 people onto universal credit in the wake of a shrinking budget(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam has her first session with her therapist, and heeds his advice on the need for closure with her relationship with Xander. Guest starring Matthew Broderick(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Sarah Smith(n)

programme.title: The Flu That Killed 50 Million
programme.desc: Docu-drama about the 1918 Flu pandemic, which killed more than 50 million people. Using dramatic reconstruction and eyewitness testimony from doctors, soldiers, civilians and politicians, this one-off brings to life the onslaught of the disease and the horrors of those who lived through it. Narrated by Christopher Eccleston(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, featuring Everton v Liverpool at Goodison Park, Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur at Meadow Park and Birmingham City v Brighton & Hove Albion at Damson Park(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Snowdonia
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the area around the summit of Snowdon in summer, finding buzzards nesting and moths being hunted by migrant nightjars from Africa. He also climbs Cadair Idris, the highest peak in the south part of the park, visits sand dunes full of orchids and spends a day on the moors watching the hen harrier.(n)

programme.title: Attenborough: 60 Years in the Wild
programme.desc: David Attenborough reflects on his TV career, charting changes in the natural world and mankind's understanding of the planet as well as developments in wildlife film-making. In the first episode, he looks back on the animal encounters that have shaped his career - from the early black-and-white Zoo Quest days to a close encounter with a lion pride in the pitch dark. He also shares a few tricks of the trade, such as how to catch a Komodo dragon and the secret to maintaining continuity in the most difficult circumstances, and recalls the making of landmark series Life on Earth(n)

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Picasso is known as the godfather of modern art. Art historian David Dibosa explores six decades of BBC archives to discover how TV has influenced our understanding of him. Dibosa reveals how filmmakers have portrayed the child genius who drew like a master, and the adult who rejected his conventional talent in a quest to reflect the world in ways that shattered all the rules(n)

programme.title: Picasso's Last Stand
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the last decade of the great artist's life, through the memories of family, close friends and the people he allowed into his world in the 1960s. As his health declined in these final years, Picasso faced damaging criticism of his work for the first time, and intimate details about his bohemian lifestyle were revealed. Yet, in the midst of disaster, he rediscovered his revolutionary spirit with a creative surge that produced some of his most sexually frank and comic work(n)

programme.title: It's True, It's True, It's True: Artemisia on Trial
programme.desc: Historical drama based on original court transcripts, that recreates the trial in 1612 of Agostino Tassi, an Italian Baroque artist who was accused of the rape of painter Artemisia Gentileschi. In order to defend himself, Tassi questioned his victim's morality, rubbished the evidence against him and nobbled the witnesses, all tactics that mirror those that are still used sometimes today. Breach Theatre's critically accalimed play blends history, myth and contemporary commentary to ask whether much has really changed in the last four centuries(n)

programme.title: Jane Austen: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Historian Lucy Worsley embarks on a road trip across England to take in some of the places Jane Austen lived in and visited during her lifetime. She aims to demonstrate how Jane's personal experiences influenced the central themes of home and property in her novels, and makes use of detective-work to recreate some of the most important locations that no longer stand. She begins in Stoneleigh Abbey, Warwickshire, retracing a journey that marked one of the many times the Austens veered wildly between wealth and genteel poverty - mirroring the Dashwoods of Sense and Sensibility. Lucy also travels from Jane's birthplace in Steventon, Hampshire, to her brother's grand estate at Godmersham Park - the inspiration for Darcy's Pemberley in Pride and Prejudice, along with Lyme Regis, Bath and Southampton(n)

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: Dr Janina Ramirez looks back at the discovery of three extraordinary pre-war treasures, and the explorer-archaeologists who came across them. In the first episode, she explores the surprise discovery in a Suffolk garden of the Sutton Hoo Hoard, an incredible Anglo-Saxon ship-burial, dating from the early 7th century AD(n)

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Picasso is known as the godfather of modern art. Art historian David Dibosa explores six decades of BBC archives to discover how TV has influenced our understanding of him. Dibosa reveals how filmmakers have portrayed the child genius who drew like a master, and the adult who rejected his conventional talent in a quest to reflect the world in ways that shattered all the rules(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Mediterranean Voyage
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto explores a shipwreck, meets Miss Montenegro and stops off in Tirana, the capital of Albania, where mayor Edi Rama has ordered the painting of many old buildings. He also visits the pirate fortress town of Ulcinj, and Duress - a poor area facing an uncertain future(n)

programme.title: A Very British Romance with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: How romance became racier following the First World War, as Marie Stopes encouraged husbands and wives to explore their sexual desires, and Edith Maude Hull's controversial novel The Sheik was published. Couples were allowed to see each other without a chaperone, while the dark environment of the cinema became a mainstay for young lovers, although old-fashioned romance would eventually rise again, as the novels of Barbara Cartland took readers back to a more innocent age(n)

programme.title: The 80s with Dominic Sandbrook
programme.desc: The historian continues his personal review of the 1980s, considering how by the middle of the decade, Britain was wrestling with two contradictory impulses - the rise of a strong nationalist sentiment and the emergence of an increasingly globalised world. Along the way Sandbrook examines everything from the invasion of the Falkland Islands to the invasion of the home computer. He examines the moral panic surrounding video nasties, looks at the Americanisation of UK culture and the picket-line skirmishes playing nightly on TV, as well as the Aids epidemic and the threat of the IRA(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary following Jewish-Israeli lawyer Lea Tsemel as she defends Palestinians being prosecuted in the nation's courts, as well as tracing her landmark cases and revealing how her political activism as a rebellious student came to shape her career as a human rights defender(n)

programme.title: Art of Germany
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores how the desire to forge a single nation of Germany found artistic expression in the Romantic movement. He travels to the coastal town of Greifswald to explore how the Baltic coast influenced Caspar David Friedrich's landscape paintings. In Berlin, he observes the art of the Prussian state that was a driving force behind the unification of Germany in 1871, and there is a look at the efforts of Franz Marc and Otto Dix to rationalise the experiences of the First World War(n)

programme.title: Treasures of the Indus
programme.desc: Sona Datta travels across the southern Indian state of Tamil Nadu to explore the development of the Hindu religion through its art and architecture. She examines its beginnings as an amalgamation of local faith traditions through to its dominant mainstream presence today, visiting sacred temples hewn from rock, sandstone and marble, including the ornately carved towers, tanks and courtyards of the Meenakshi Amman Temple in Madurai(n)

programme.title: Sylvia Plath: Life Inside the Bell Jar
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on Sylvia Plath's semi-autobiographical novel, exploring the wider landscape of 1950s gender politics and Plath's own struggles to survive society's constraints. Featuring an interview with the author's daughter Frieda Hughes, who discusses her mother in depth on camera for the first time, as well as impressionistic reconstructions of excerpts from the book featuring readings by Maggie Gyllenhaal, unpublished letters, photographs and archive materials, and contributions from people who knew the writer(n)

programme.title: A Very British Romance with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: How romance became racier following the First World War, as Marie Stopes encouraged husbands and wives to explore their sexual desires, and Edith Maude Hull's controversial novel The Sheik was published. Couples were allowed to see each other without a chaperone, while the dark environment of the cinema became a mainstay for young lovers, although old-fashioned romance would eventually rise again, as the novels of Barbara Cartland took readers back to a more innocent age(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world. With Katty Kay and Christian Fraser(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford examines the story of Andreas Vesalius, who in 1537 became the most famous anatomist in Europe. He went on to produce the first complete account of the human body and how to dissect it, and his drawings set the standard for anatomical art for centuries to come, earning him the title of 'the founder of modern anatomy'(n)

programme.title: London: A Tale of Two Cities with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: The historian examines how London coped with a series of disastrous events in the 17th century, including the Great Fire, the Plague and the Civil War, and became one of Europe's most powerful cities. He uses information from surveys taken by chroniclers at the beginning and end of the century to explore the qualities that enabled Londoners to thrive, and explains how events of the period laid the foundations for the English capital's success(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Tomb on Earth: Secrets of Ancient China
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Alice Roberts and Albert Lin explore the final resting place of China's first emperor, a site 200 times larger than Egypt's Valley of the Kings and home to the famous Terracotta Warriors. Making use of both the latest forensic technology and ancient texts, they delve into this historic site's secrets and uncover evidence that casts a new light on the founding of the nation(n)

programme.title: Contagion: The BBC Four Pandemic
programme.desc: Mathematician Hannah Fry and emergency medic Dr Javid Abdelmoneim front this experiment to test the threat of a new flu pandemic to the UK. An app is used to 'infect' users and track their movements and social interactions over 24 hours, with Hannah adopting the role of Patient Zero as she walks the streets of Haslemere in Surrey to launch the outbreak, while Javid investigates why flu is such a danger to society and visits a factory that will produce vaccine when the next virus emerges(n)

programme.title: Reporting History: Mandela and a New South Africa
programme.desc: On the 25th anniversary of Mandela's election as South African president, Fergal Keane goes back to examine his reports and considers why history did not turn out how he expected(n)

programme.desc: Major Clanger invents a machine for cleaning up the planet. When he gets called away by Granny, Small and Tiny try it for themselves - and soon get into difficulty. Narrated by Michael Palin(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy sings with a megaphone in the garden, and wants to carry on instead of going to bed or playing with Igglepiggle. Narrated by Derek Jacobi(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Eve Myles reads The Dawn Chorus by Suzanne Barton, the tale of a tiny nightingale desperate to sing with the flock of birds whose music enchants him every morning(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures - Everton v Crystal Palace at Goodison Park and Brighton & Hove Albion v Watford at AMEX Stadium. Everton were unbeaten in their last nine meetings with the Eagles, but the sides could not be separated when they met at Selhurst Park in August. Brighton recorded a comfortable 3-0 away win in the reverse fixture against the Hornets, but Watford had shown signs of improvement under Nigel Pearson(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: James Williams presents the latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the studio audience at Oasis Academy MediaCityUK in Salford(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, his wife Erin and their two children sample life in Melbourne for a week, hoping the move would improve their work-life balance and quality of family time(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks offer advice to two teams at the Highland Centre in Edinburgh, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to a camera obscura in the city(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher looks at faith and autism, meeting a minister with Asperger's syndrome and hearing from members of the world's first performing autistic adult choir(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson goes property hunting in rural Wiltshire with a couple who have £950,000 to buy a family home with land, where they and their young children can enjoy a self-sufficient lifestyle. Jules also takes time out to find out about the county's glove-making heritage and sees some famous examples made for famous figures(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v Italy (Kick-off 3.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the concluding match from the second round of fixtures, held at Stade de France. The hosts will be in confident mood after their impressive performance against England in their opening match, while Italy were comfortably routed by Wales. The French prevailed 25-14 in Italy last year to extend their winning run against the Azzurri in this competition to six matches. With analysis from Thomas Castaignede, Sergio Parisse and Joe Worsley, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Jonathan Davies and Paul O'Connell(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television, here discovering how to look good. It's a story of bodybuilding, beauty queens and leather bikinis, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker, Charlotte Smith and Steve Brown head to the Auchlyne Estate near Killin in Perthshire, the first of four visits they will be making over the next year to chart life there throughout the seasons. Plus, Tom Heap goes back to the classroom to see if the next generation of farmers are being given the training they need(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: From ancient Syria to present day Sheffield and out into the wilds of space, something is stalking the Doctor and her friends. As Graham, Yaz and Ryan return home to see friends and family, they find themselves haunted by very different experiences. Who is the figure calling from beyond the stars for help, and what are the fearsome Chagaskas terrorising Aleppo in 1380? To find the answers, the Doctor and her companions must embark on a mission that forces them to face their darkest fears(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: At the Turners, Shelagh receives a phone call from the Hong Kong adoption agency, which has the alarming news that someone from May's past has arrived in the UK and wants to see the little girl. Meanwhile, Valerie's cousin Maureen is heavily pregnant and hoping her baby will be born on Thursday, the same date as her father and grandfather. However, her due date isn't so soon and keen to bring on the birth, Maureen takes a big risk by secretly taking castor oil - with potentially disastrous consequences(n)

programme.title: The Pale Horse
programme.desc: Widowed antique dealer Mark Easterbrook investigates when his name appears on a list found in the shoe of a dead woman, and his enquiries lead him to the home of three spinsters in Much Deeping. The village is a place of old traditions, macabre rituals and strange beliefs, a place of witches, curses and spells, and as more deaths occur Mark starts to fear for his own life and sanity. First of a two-part adaptation of Agatha Christie's 1961 novel, starring Rufus Sewell, Sean Pertwee and Kaya Scodelario(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Manchester City v West Ham United at Etihad Stadium and Sheffield United v Bournemouth at Bramall Lane. West Ham were looking to avenge a 5-0 thrashing at the hands of the Citizens on the opening weekend of the season, while Sheffield United were aiming to continue their impressive campaign back in the top flight by inflicting more misery upon the struggling Cherries(n)

programme.desc: Lucy Owen visits a Prestatyn gym to find out what our rights are when fitness instructors and personal trainers let clients down. Rachel Treadaway-Williams meets customers left disappointed by a company that converts historic buildings into homes(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Callum prepares for his assessment and gets much-needed reassurance from Karen and Stuart - only to be thrown by the arrival of an unexpected visitor. Linda and Mick put on a united front with the social worker, and Sonia invites Whitney on a weekend away - unaware Leo is eavesdropping from the attic. Bobby suggests that Bex should move in with him, Iqra and Habiba, and Rainie gives Daniel a piece of her mind about his fake funeral plans(n)

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: When a head-on crash leaves a grandfather dead, Chris discovers the answers could lie in electronic data within the car. As his mission to access it begins, he unearths an intriguing story of what really led up to the incident(n)

programme.title: Rio and Kate: Becoming a Stepfamily
programme.desc: Documentary following former England captain Rio Ferdinand and his fiancee Kate Wright in the months leading up to their wedding as she integrates into the family and becomes a stepmother to his three children Lorenz, Tate and Tia. Kate takes on not only the role as Rio's new partner, but also of parent to three children still grieving over the death of their mother Rebecca in 2015 and grandmother Janet two years later(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Callie Lewis was just 24 when she took her own life. She had been in the care of the NHS, but fell through the cracks of an understaffed and overstretched system. For the last 16 months, Panorama has been filming with her family as they tried to understand what went wrong. Shortly before she died, they learned that Callie was a member of an online suicide forum, where she found detailed advice on how to kill herself. As reporter Ellie Flynn discovers, Callie's death comes at a time when many people with mental health issues continue to complain about their struggle to connect with the services they need(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam lies to Jess about missing his mental-health assessment and tensions in their relationship reach breaking point, leaving him suddenly nostalgic for the simplicity of first love(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets Hollywood star Mark Ruffalo, who is currently appearing in new legal thriller Dark Waters. Friends star David Schwimmer and British comedian and writer Nick Mohammed discuss their new spy comedy Intelligence. Episodes star Tamsin Greig also appears to talk about her role in Julian Fellowes' new period drama, Belgravia. Plus, there's music from singer-songwriter Alicia Keys, who performs her new single Underdog, while more intrepid audience members attempt to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda berates Mick for talking to the social worker about her and sets out to put the woman straight, while Tina questions whether Ollie is safe around his mother, leaving Mick conflicted. Whitney opens up to Kush about a letter she kept from Tony, leaving Leo intrigued as he listens in. Sonia is distraught to learn that Bex is moving out, Sharon and Dennis finally reach an understanding and Jean is upset when Daniel snaps at her(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Cameron plans to give Chloe his mother's necklace as a Valentine's Day gift - but first he needs to get it back from Nicky. She has problems of her own - she's pawned Bernie's necklace to pay her mother's debts, but is still being harassed for more money, forcing her to put her life and career on the line. Sacha is troubled by a personal decision, but receives guidance from an old friend(n)

programme.title: Hidden
programme.desc: Cadi and Vaughan are called to investigate when a body is discovered in a house outside Blaenau Ffestiniog. programme.title: The Split

Nathan takes on a protegee, and the firm takes on two high-profile clients - a TV presenter locked in an unhappy and controlling marriage and a man who wants a divorce before he dies. Guest starring Donna Air(n)

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Caitlin tries to keep her family calm, but is furious to discover Ellie might have unwittingly drawn attention to the place they have hidden Joe's body. Meanwhile, Lenny fumes when a Valentine's card arrives from an impossible sender, Eve reluctantly decides to host a ladies' night, and Dylan tries to find out the identity of his secret admirer(n)

programme.title: The Split
programme.desc: Return of the legal drama, starring Nicola Walker. A death in the family brings everyone back together. While Hannah's marriage seems to be back on track, she has begun a dangerous affair with Christie. Zander returns from America, and introduces an unsettling new addition to the team. Nathan takes on a protegee, and the firm takes on two high-profile clients - a TV presenter locked in an unhappy and controlling marriage and a man who wants a divorce before he dies. Sacha is troubled by a personal decision, but receives guidance from an old friend(n)

programme.desc: Four women watch live breast reduction as they consider going under the knife. They share their reasons for thinking about surgery, many of which are often more practical than aesthetic, including back pain and limitations on what they can do with their children(n)

programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures - Everton v Crystal Palace at Goodison Park and Brighton & Hove Albion v Watford at AMEX Stadium. Everton were unbeaten in their last nine meetings with the Eagles, but the sides could not be separated when they met at Selhurst Park in August. Brighton recorded a comfortable 3-0 away win in the reverse fixture against the Hornets, but Watford had shown signs of improvement under Nigel Pearson(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)
